objective: Close F-0126 statically by capturing an immutable server-owned organization-training recipient snapshot in the canonical publish transaction, deriving dashboard and employee denominators from that snapshot before left joining official submissions, and distinguishing authorized zero data from legacy snapshot unavailability and permission denial.
blockedFiles: all schema and migration files except the five exact schema/migration paths in allowedFiles
any migration apply, real database/data access, backfill, UPDATE/DELETE/DROP/TRUNCATE or destructive DDL
any role, entitlement, organization scope, Provider, quota, dependency, UI, export, deployment, secret/env, external service, PR, force-push or mechanism change

preRedImpactScan.authoritativeChain: canonical organization-training publish transaction -> immutable version recipient snapshot -> authorized organization scope and version-active interval -> exact employee/version denominator -> left-joined official submissions -> dashboard and database-paginated employee summaries
preRedImpactScan.currentVulnerableSink: the production analytics source reads only submitted answers, derives eligible employees and visible versions from those same answers, returns null for zero submissions and therefore reports 100 percent completion or false access denial
preRedImpactScan.securityInvariant: authorization and visible organization scope are resolved before analytics facts; each current version uses an immutable publish-time recipient snapshot; legacy or corrupt snapshot data is explicitly unavailable and never guessed from mutable current membership; only summary projections leave the repository
preRedImpactScan.legitimateBehavior: authorized zero eligible recipients or zero submissions returns success with zero counts and an empty employee page; taken-down and deadline-bounded versions use their historical active interval; submitted answers remain attributed by their captured organization snapshot
preRedImpactScan.authorizationSemantics: existing advanced org_auth scope only; no expansion

requiredSemantics.authorization: existing effective advanced org_auth, actor role and visible organization scope must be established before version, recipient, employee or answer reads; invalid or cross-scope callers receive the existing masked denial and no fact query
requiredSemantics.schema: add exactly four legacy-nullable markers to organization_training_version: recipient_snapshot_schema_version, recipient_snapshot_captured_at, recipient_snapshot_count and recipient_snapshot_digest; legacy requires all four null, current requires all four non-null with schemaVersion=1, non-negative integer count and lowercase SHA-256 digest; add one internal append-only organization_training_version_recipient table with a restrict version FK, employeePublicId, captured organizationPublicId, exact recipient orgAuth authorizationPublicId and createdAt, no mutable employee/org_auth cascade FK, unique version plus employee and application-level case-conflict rejection
requiredSemantics.publish: the canonical publish transaction first validates draft/version organization, publish scope, profession, level, subject and publisher authorization, then derives recipients from one authoritative active employee/user plus valid recipient org_auth query; recipient authorizationPublicId is the employee recipient source and is not the publisher/version authorizationPublicId; multiple candidates must use an existing explicit priority or fail closed; version markers, ordinal-canonical recipient rows and draft consumption use one controlled time anchor and one transaction, and replay verifies the original snapshot without recapture
requiredSemantics.immutability: recipient rows have no mutation or delete API and are not recomputed after employee transfer, unbind, account disable, org_auth expiry or training takedown; replay of the same publish operation returns the original snapshot without recapture
requiredSemantics.interval: a training version participates in a selected inclusive date range only when publishedAt is at or before range endAt and effectiveEnd, the earliest non-null answerDeadlineAt or takenDownAt, is at or after range startAt; both null means no upper bound; effectiveEnd before publishedAt or other temporal corruption makes the read unavailable; official submissions must be inside both the request range and the version publishedAt-to-effectiveEnd interval
requiredSemantics.scope: analytics filters captured recipient organizationPublicId by the already-authorized requested organization plus descendants; numerator joins exact versionPublicId, employeePublicId, captured organizationPublicId and recipient authorizationPublicId and validates the answer organization snapshot; sibling, ancestor, out-of-scope, different authorization lineage, transferred-current-organization or wrong-version submissions never enter counts
requiredSemantics.integrity: for every relevant current version, schemaVersion, capturedAt, count, canonical recipient set and SHA-256 digest must agree exactly; one versioned unambiguous canonicalizer hashes schemaVersion plus the full employeePublicId-ordinal-sorted tuples of employeePublicId, captured organizationPublicId and recipient authorizationPublicId; public ids are non-empty, bounded and ordinal case-sensitive; duplicate, case-conflicting, missing, extra, cross-scope or identity-mismatched rows make the whole analytics read unavailable rather than partially filtering
requiredSemantics.legacy: a relevant legacy version with all-null recipient snapshot markers returns a stable temporary-unavailable response after authorization, never access denied, zero, a current-roster reconstruction or a partial metric; no backfill or inferred history is allowed
requiredSemantics.denominator: dashboard eligible employees and each employee visible training version set come exclusively from complete recipient snapshots; official submitted answers are left joined by exact version and employee identity, so no submission is represented as unfinished rather than absent
requiredSemantics.emptyState: no relevant version, a valid zero-recipient snapshot or zero official submission is a successful zero/empty analytics state; legacy or corrupt snapshot uses a stable temporary-unavailable code and message distinct from permission denial; null is never reused to encode authorization denial, unavailability and valid empty data
requiredSemantics.pagination: employee total, deterministic ordering, page rows and visible-version facts use the same recipient predicate and remain database-backed; page slicing is not performed after an unbounded application read
requiredSemantics.privacy: the internal recipient snapshot is never added to organization-training or analytics DTOs, logs, errors or UI; existing summary-only redaction remains and raw answers, AI content, Prompt, Provider payload, internal ids and cross-scope facts remain unavailable
requiredSemantics.migration: offline Drizzle generation only; SQL may contain only additive nullable marker columns, the recipient table, exact indexes/checks/foreign key and tool metadata; no apply, backfill, data mutation or destructive statement
requiredSemantics.findingDisposition: F-0126 may close only when new-version immutable denominators, legacy-unavailable behavior, zero-submission success, employee pagination and adversarial transfer/unbind/takedown/date-boundary matrices are all GREEN; runtime DB proof remains explicitly unclaimed

minimumAdversarialTests: 0, 2, 4, 5 and 10 captured recipients with 0, partial and complete submissions produce truthful counts, unfinished values, completion rates and small-sample inputs

publish captures only exact active employee and org_auth scope; cross-organization, wrong profession or level, inactive user, expired/cancelled auth and duplicate/case-conflicting recipient facts fail closed
publish marker, recipient insert or draft consume failure rolls back the whole transaction; replay returns the original recipient snapshot without recapture
employee transfer, authorization unbind, account disable, org_auth expiry and later organization-tree changes do not rewrite the captured snapshot or reinterpret historical recipient organization
taken-down, deadline-ended and range-boundary versions follow the exact inclusive interval contract

legacy all-null marker is unavailable; partial marker, count/digest mismatch, duplicate/cross-scope/cross-authorization recipient and corrupt submitted answer identity fail closed without partial metrics
authorized no-version, valid zero-recipient and zero-submission states return success 0/empty; unauthorized and cross-scope requests perform zero analytics fact reads
dashboard and employee page use the same denominator; database count/order/page remain consistent and unanswered employees remain present
network DTO and serialized responses contain no recipient snapshot, internal id, raw answer, Prompt, Provider payload or other blocked field
existing organization-training publish/idempotency/takedown/employee visibility and organization analytics authorization/redaction regressions remain GREEN

validationCommands.name: state-and-schema-source-contract
arguments: validates the F-0126 task state and additive recipient snapshot migration source
